steps.title: Identify the pattern to explain
steps.content: The passage shows a parallel: immigrants from Mandarin-speaking northern China settled in Flushing and speak Mandarin; immigrants from Cantonese/Fuzhounese-speaking southern China settled in Chinatown and speak those languages. The correct answer must explain why settlement location tracks home-region language.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Chinese immigrants who emigrated to New York City many years ago are more likely to speak several Chinese languages than are more recent Chinese immigrants.": Discusses multilingualism over time, which doesn't explain why a specific language maps to a specific neighborhood. ✗
  • Choice B — "There are geographic differences in primary language within China that are reflected in the settlement patterns of Chinese immigrants in New York City.": Directly explains the correlation: because different regions of China speak different languages, immigrants from those regions brought their languages with them and clustered together in the same NYC neighborhoods. ✓
  • Choice C — "Chinese immigrants regularly change their residences between Queens and Manhattan after they emigrate, rather than staying in one borough.": Suggests fluidity of residence, which would undermine the stable language-neighborhood pattern the passage describes. ✗
  • Choice D — "Taken together, there are more Cantonese and Fuzhounese speakers among Chinese immigrants in New York City than there are Mandarin speakers.": A population count that explains nothing about why particular languages cluster in particular neighborhoods. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
